batchEvaluateConstraints(uint256[],uint2.details: Evaluates the AIR constraint polynomial at given points

params.weights: Array of weights for each constraint

params.dcpX: DCP value at domain point x
params.dcpZ: DCP value at out-of-domain point z

computeDeepComposition(uint256,uint256,u.details: DCP combines trace and constraint polynomials at out-of-domain point

evaluatePolynomial(uint256[],uint256).details: coefficients[i] represents the coefficient of x^i

returns.result: The polynomial value at the point

verifyDegreeBound(uint256[],uint256,uint.details: Checks that constraint evaluations are consistent with degree bound

returns.valid: True if degree bound is satisfied

batchEvaluateConstraints(uint256[],uint2.notice: Evaluate constraints at multiple domain points

composeConstraints(uint256[],uint256).notice: Compose multiple constraint values using a random challenge

evaluatePolynomial(uint256[],uint256).notice: Evaluate polynomial at a given point using Horner's method

generateChallenge(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Generate challenge from transcript using SHA3-256

verifyDegreeBound(uint256[],uint256,uint.notice: Verify constraint polynomial has degree within bound

@notice Domain separator for constraint hashing

@notice Compute modular inverse using Fermat's little theorem
 @dev a^(-1) = a^(p-2) mod p for prime p
